methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Valinor Enterprises is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Trae Stephens and Grant Verstandig, having raised $85M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers—8 private and 2 public companies in the same sector and stage, including Series B firms such as Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), and Castelion ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $85M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implying $170M. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $125M to $3.0B. The median peer is substantially higher than the stage-progression result, reflecting the capital intensity and risk profile of the defense tech sector.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $144M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median point of $585M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Valinor's revenue traction, customer concentration, and development timelines—typical for pre-revenue or early-revenue frontier defense companies. The peer-median substantially exceeds stage-progression, suggesting either elevated investor appetite for the sector or material differentiation among the comparable set that warrants further due diligence on product maturity and customer validation.
